what_it_does: Defines TypeThumb, a universal thumbnail renderer for any Type in the CV_REGISTRY. It dispatches by layer/family/runtime to a specific preview renderer (token color/type/shape, atom icon/status/shape/stamp, block, system widget, surface widget/wizard-step/slide, doc widget/wizard/deck/brochure, template), and uses a ScaledThumb helper that renders children at native size then transform-scales them to fit a target box. Block/system/slide/deck/brochure previews render THROUGH the shared engine (window.__cvRenderType) gated on window.__coreReady; other previews are hand-drawn mock chrome. Exposes TypeThumb, ScaledThumb, RenderShape, ThumbPlaceholder on window.
observations: ScaledThumb computes a uniform scale = min(sx,sy) and translate to center (or top-align) native content inside the box, with pointerEvents:none on the scaled layer
pickRenderer is a layer→family dispatch table returning a render function; returns null (→ ThumbPlaceholder) for unknown layers
Block/System/Slide/Deck/Brochure renderers each carry the same ready-gate pattern: useState seeded from window.__cvRenderType, useEffect awaiting window.__coreReady then setReady
Cross-doc embed blocks (embedWidget/embedWizard) intentionally bypass the core engine and use window.WS_BLOCKS[key].render or window.WidgetRender instead
MOCK_WIDGET_DOC is a shared inline fixture (Tower East dashboard: kpis, rows, media, spark chart, cta) reused by system/surface/doc widget previews
RenderShape draws hexagon/octagon/diamond/circle as inline SVG using var(--accent-gold) / var(--accent-gold-soft)
isLightHex computes perceived luminance (r*299+g*587+b*114)/1000 > 150 to pick readable text color over a swatch, expanding 3-digit hex first
TemplateThumb recursively renders the parent Type's thumbnail plus a variable-count badge
DocWizardThumb resolves steps via explicit defaults > legacy window.WIZARD_KINDS lookup > a hardcoded 5-step fallback (Capture/Enrich/Audience/Review/Publish)
React hooks are aliased with _th suffix (useMemo_th etc.) destructured from global React
standouts: Inline comments tie renderers to named program axes: UNIFICATION W3 (render through the one engine), REQUIREMENTS F2 (product UI = containment tree), W7 (dataviz atom pending)
The async readiness gate is explicitly described as 'readiness, not a fallback' — the preview waits for the real engine rather than substituting a fake
Deck/brochure previews render the actual FIRST slide through the engine on a stacked-card affordance, single-sourced from archetype defaults
conventions: Follows the project rule that previews render through the one engine (window.__cvRenderType) rather than a hand-drawn copy where possible
Reads everything from window.CV_REGISTRY / window globals rather than imports (browser-global module style)
Uses var(--token) for all colors/elevation/zones, no raw hex in chrome (hex only appears as live swatch data)
Attaches public API via Object.assign(window, {...}) at end of file
concerns: Hardcoded fallback wizard steps and MOCK_WIDGET_DOC are inline literal fixtures living in this preview file rather than referenced from a single home
The ready-gate useEffect is duplicated verbatim across five renderers (Block/System/Slide/Deck/Brochure) with [ready] deps
TokenColorThumb default hex '#E0C010' is a raw literal in a consumer rather than a token reference
notes: The universal Type→preview dispatcher: real engine output for block/system/slide/deck/brochure (gated on core readiness), hand-drawn chrome for tokens/atoms/wizards.
questions: Where is window.__cvRenderType / window.__coreReady set, and what guarantees the gate resolves?
Are the inline fallback wizard steps and MOCK_WIDGET_DOC intentionally local fixtures or should they reference a shared sample-data home?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header states a layer-aware universal thumbnail renderer used in TypeCard everywhere, and the file delivers exactly that dispatch-plus-scale system.
apparent_themes: Render-through-the-one-engine (preview IS engine output, single source of truth)
Layer/family/runtime dispatch over a typed registry
intention_signals: Trying to make every Type previewable from one place so a built-in OR Vi-proposed Type shows a faithful preview, with the preview being the actual engine output rather than a separate mock that could drift.
novelty: The deliberate split — engine-rendered previews for composable layers vs. hand-drawn mock chrome for tokens/atoms/wizards — plus the 'readiness not fallback' async gate and the recursive template-thumbnail-with-parent pattern.
contribution: Contributes a complete universal Type-to-thumbnail rendering system: the ScaledThumb fit-to-box mechanic, the full layer/family dispatch table, the readiness-gated engine-rendering pattern, the inline preview fixtures, and SVG shape/luminance helpers — the visual preview face of the Type registry.
summary_for_embedding: A React/browser-global universal thumbnail renderer (TypeThumb) that dispatches any registry Type by layer/family to engine-rendered or hand-drawn previews, scaled to fit, with an async core-readiness gate.
intention_for_embedding: To give every Type in the registry one faithful preview surface so previews are the real engine output and cannot drift from what the Type actually renders.
